# Cross-Service Flows

<!-- generated by rebuild-spec synthesize_system — {{timestamp}} -->
<!-- snapshot-hash: {{snapshot_hash}} -->

> **[INCREMENTAL]** — Tier-2 artifact. Populate after interaction-graph and service-catalog
> are validated on a real project.
>
> Documents saga/journey flows that span multiple services (e.g. order → payment →
> inventory → notify). Static analysis can surface the edges; saga *ordering* and
> *compensating transactions* require human authorship or distributed-tracing runtime data.

## Flow: {{flow_name}}

**Trigger:** {{trigger_description}}
**Initiating service:** {{initiating_service}}
**Outcome:** {{expected_outcome}}

| Step | Service | Action | Message / Topic | Next Step | On Failure |
|------|---------|--------|-----------------|-----------|------------|
| 1 | {{service}} | {{action}} | {{rpc_or_topic}} | Step 2 | {{compensation}} |
| 2 | {{service}} | {{action}} | {{rpc_or_topic}} | Step 3 | {{compensation}} |
| N | {{service}} | {{action}} | — | end | — |

**Notes:**
- Steps derived from static edge analysis are `[UNVERIFIED]` until runtime-confirmed.
- Compensating transactions (sagas) must be authored manually — static analysis cannot
  recover rollback semantics.

---

## Adding New Flows

Copy the table above for each distinct user journey. Reference edge labels from
`interaction-graph.md` to stay consistent. Mark steps as `[UNVERIFIED]` until confirmed
by distributed tracing (e.g. OpenTelemetry / Jaeger span data).
